response.justification: While this is a talented and mature software developer with strong engineering practices and communication skills, there is a critical mismatch on both mandatory requirements: only ~6 months of C++ experience (vs. 5–10 years required) and zero exposure to financial services or capital markets domains. The candidate's 16-year career has been primarily in web, backend, and Linux-based systems, with no evidence of Windows platform development or the specialized domain knowledge essential for this capital markets role.
strengths: Mature developer with 16+ years of experience and demonstrated software craftsmanship (TDD, CI/CD, clean code practices)
Java development background and strong CI/CD infrastructure expertise, complementary to the role
Excellent communication abilities; has led workshops, open-source projects, and mentored teams
concerns: Only ~6 months of C++ experience (National Instruments 2014–2015) vs. mandatory 5–10 years requirement
Zero demonstrated experience in financial services, capital markets, fixed-income, or investment banking domains
Career focused on web backends, microservices, and Linux; no evidence of Windows platform development expertise
